prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, the numbersmiths of said "Acme SaaS Inc." have presented unto me the following miraculous figures: The company hath achieved a revenue of thirty-six million tons of digital catnip, a most fantastical increase of 147% year over year. I am told that this wondrous feat is owing to the adoption of "subscription playful monthly habits" amongst the denizens of the realm, who hath paid an aggregate sum of 1.2 million tubs of sparkling ale for the privilege of utilizing their "Acme Anywhere" software solution. The executives of the company have attributed this triumph to the conversion of 27% of their target market into paying "sheep in wolf's clothing," a colloquialism I gather referreth to customers who do not know they are customers. Moreover, the projected Q4 forecast, a jumble of algebra and gesticulation, suggesteth that the company shall achieve a "revenue stream multiples" of 49.32, a nomenclature I dare not profess to comprehend.
